title: How to Identify Luxury Buildings Worth Your Time
dek: Not all luxury buildings are equal business opportunities. Some will feed you deals for a decade; others will consume your energy and produce nothing. Here's how to tell the difference before you invest a single month.
heroAlt: How to Identify Luxury Buildings Worth Your Time

blocks.html: A 400-unit tower with heavy one-bedroom inventory turns over far more leases per year than a boutique 40-unit building. More turnover means more move-ins, more overflow renters, and more chances for your phone to ring. Unit count and unit mix are the first filter.

Call and ask two questions: do you co-op with outside agents, and what's your commission policy? Buildings split roughly into three camps: agent-friendly (welcome, standard co-op), agent-neutral (tolerated), and agent-hostile. Only the first two belong on your list.

blocks.text: Never fight for a locked door when the building next door is holding theirs open.

blocks.html: Your commission is roughly one month's rent, so the building's average rent is effectively your rate card. A building averaging $3,800 pays nearly double one averaging $2,000 for identical work. Work the tier your market supports and your reputation can grow into.

Slow application processing, chaotic leasing offices, and bad resident reviews kill deals you've already won. Read the reviews, note how fast the office answers, watch how they treat walk-ins. You're evaluating a business partner, because that's what they'll be.
Five great buildings within a fifteen-minute walk beat eight great buildings across three suburbs. Your touch rhythm, showings, and reputation all concentrate when your buildings do.

Score every candidate on these five, keep the top twenty-five, and you've built the foundation of the whole business. The full system is in my free ebook Luxury Leases, Real Profits. Better agents win.
